questions.q: Who gave Karna the Vasavi Shakti?
questions.explanation: Indra granted Karna the infallible dart in exchange for his birth armor and earrings.

questions.q: What did Karna surrender to obtain the Vasavi Shakti?
options: A vow never to fight Arjuna
questions.explanation: Karna cut away his kavacha and kundala, born with his body, and gave them to Indra.

questions.q: How many times could Karna use the Vasavi Shakti?
questions.explanation: The dart would slay one foe, however mighty, and then return to Indra forever.

questions.q: For whom was Karna saving the Vasavi Shakti?
questions.explanation: Karna hoarded the dart for his destined duel with Arjuna.

questions.q: On whom was Karna finally forced to use the dart?
questions.explanation: Pressed by the Kauravas on the fourteenth night, Karna loosed the Shakti at the rampaging Ghatotkacha.

questions.q: Who was Ghatotkacha, the dart's victim?
options: A Naga prince allied to the Pandavas
questions.explanation: Ghatotkacha was Bhima's half-rakshasa son, a terror in night battle.

questions.q: In what disguise did Indra approach Karna for the armor?
questions.explanation: Indra came as a brahmin at Karna's prayer hour, when Karna refused no one.

questions.q: What happened to the Vasavi Shakti after it killed its single target?
options: Karna reclaimed it from the corpse
questions.explanation: Its one use spent, the blazing dart streaked back to Indra in heaven.

questions.q: The name 'Vasavi Shakti' comes from which name of Indra?
questions.explanation: Vasava, lord of the Vasus, is Indra; the Shakti is 'Vasava's dart'.

questions.q: During which battle phase did Karna hurl the Shakti at Ghatotkacha?
questions.explanation: Ghatotkacha's rakshasa power swelled at night, forcing Karna's hand in the darkness after the fourteenth day.

questions.q: Why was Karna's armor so precious that Indra schemed for it?
options: He was born with it, fused to his body, making him unslayable
It could deflect every astra but one
questions.explanation: The kavacha and kundala, born of Surya's essence, made Karna effectively invulnerable in battle.

questions.q: Who forewarned Karna of Indra's plan to beg away his armor?
questions.explanation: His father Surya appeared in a dream, warning that Indra would come disguised as a brahmin.

questions.q: What did Surya advise Karna to do, and how did Karna respond?
options: Refuse the gift; Karna agreed reluctantly
Refuse the beggar; Karna chose generosity over safety
Flee the encounter; Karna stood his ground
Demand the Shakti first; Karna forgot the advice
questions.explanation: Karna would not tarnish his vow of giving, accepting doom rather than refuse a supplicant.

questions.q: How did Karna remove the armor that was part of his body?
options: He cut it from his flesh
Indra dissolved it with a mantra
It fell away at Surya's command
He burned it off in sacred fire
questions.explanation: Karna sliced the kavacha and kundala from his own body and handed them over, bleeding.

questions.q: What earned Karna the name 'Vaikartana'?
options: His mastery of the Vijaya bow
His cutting away of his own armor
questions.explanation: Vaikartana, 'the cutter', commemorates his carving off the armor — the name also ties him to Surya.

questions.q: Why did Krishna deliberately send Ghatotkacha against Karna that night?
options: To exhaust the Shakti and save Arjuna
To break the Kaurava night formation
questions.explanation: Krishna knew Karna would be forced to spend his one-use dart, removing the deadliest threat to Arjuna.

questions.q: How did Krishna react to Ghatotkacha's death?
options: He rejoiced, to the Pandavas' shock
He hid the news from Bhima
questions.explanation: Krishna danced with relief, explaining that with the Shakti spent, Arjuna was as good as saved.

questions.q: What did the dying Ghatotkacha do with his final act?
options: Hurled his mace into Karna's chariot
Enlarged his body to crush a vast part of the Kaurava army
Called on Bhima to avenge him
questions.explanation: Falling, the giant expanded his frame and annihilated a great division of the Kaurava host beneath him.

questions.q: Under what pressure did Karna finally abandon his plan to save the dart?
options: Duryodhana and the panicked Kaurava ranks begged him to stop Ghatotkacha
questions.explanation: With the night army crumbling under rakshasa sorcery, Duryodhana's desperate pleas forced Karna's hand.

questions.q: What name is the Vasavi Shakti also known by, denoting that it cannot miss?
questions.explanation: Amogha — unfailing — names the dart's certainty: one cast, one kill.

questions.q: What condition did Indra attach when granting the Shakti?
options: It must never target a kshatriya
It would work only at night
It would slay one enemy, then return to Indra
Karna must never speak of the exchange
questions.explanation: Indra balanced his boon: matchless power, but for a single use only.

questions.q: How does the epic weigh Indra's trade with Karna morally?
options: As a god's rightful test of mortals
As generosity so great it shamed the king of gods into a counter-gift
questions.explanation: Indra, moved and abashed by Karna's unhesitating gift, could not leave without granting something in return.

questions.q: Whose son was Indra protecting by stripping Karna of his armor?
questions.explanation: Indra, father of Arjuna, schemed to blunt Karna before the destined duel.

questions.q: On which day-boundary of the war did the Shakti claim Ghatotkacha?
options: The night after the twelfth day
The night after the fourteenth day
The night before the war began
questions.explanation: The battle uniquely raged past sunset after day fourteen, the night of Ghatotkacha's rampage.

questions.q: What had made Karna's position desperate enough to use the dart, given rakshasa night-power?
options: Ghatotkacha's illusions were slaughtering the army and no astra checked him
The dart was fading with each sunrise
questions.explanation: Maya-warfare in darkness made Ghatotkacha nearly unstoppable; only the infallible dart could end it.

questions.q: What did Karna lose strategically the instant the Shakti left his hand?
options: His right to command the army
questions.explanation: The dart was his one certain answer to Arjuna; spending it foreshadowed his fall on day seventeen.

questions.q: How does Krishna later describe Ghatotkacha's death to the grieving Pandavas?
options: As a sacrifice he himself engineered for the greater victory
questions.explanation: Krishna admits he steered events so the Shakti would be spent on Ghatotkacha rather than Arjuna.

questions.q: In some tellings, why could the Shakti not simply be re-granted to Karna later?
options: Its single-use nature was the boon's unbreakable condition
Brahma reclaimed it for the gods' armory
questions.explanation: The weapon's covenant was fixed at one use; even Indra could not amend it.

questions.q: Which two epithets of Karna both trace to the armor episode and his solar birth?
questions.explanation: Vaikartana recalls the cutting of the armor and the sun, while Vasusena was his childhood name, born with wealth — the armor itself.

questions.q: What broader Mahabharata theme does the Vasavi Shakti episode most embody?
options: Generosity and fate entwined — a gift that doomed the giver
The triumph of deception over honor
questions.explanation: Karna's unmatched charity cost him invulnerability and, through Krishna's design, his surest weapon — generosity braided into tragedy.
